steps.title: Combine the two findings
steps.content: Hart found a significant increase in investment during the three years after liberalization. Collins and Menon found no enduring increase over a longer period. The completion must explain both a short-term rise and a long-term lack of persistence.

steps.title: Choose the fading-effect claim
steps.content: A says growth is consistent, which conflicts with the nonenduring result. B introduces expectations the passage never mentions. C reverses the timeline by delaying benefits until after three years. The combined findings point to a temporary boost. The answer is D.
